message:
fix: correct board view column width calculation to prevent line rendering glitch (#114)

The board view had misaligned box-drawing characters and broken vertical
borders when the terminal was narrower than the total desired column
width. Three bugs in the width calculation caused columns to overflow:

1. Border overhead was not included in the width budget. Each column
   uses lipgloss RoundedBorder() which adds 2 characters outside the
   Width() value, but the calculation only subtracted inter-column
   "gaps" that did not actually exist in the rendering.

2. The minimum column width was enforced as a hard floor (28 chars)
   even when that would cause total width to exceed the terminal.
   Columns now shrink gracefully down to 12 chars when needed.

3. Header width did not match the column body width (off by the border
   overhead), causing misalignment between headers and column borders.

The fix computes baseWidth = (boardWidth / numCols) - borderOverhead,
verifies the total fits, and aligns headers to match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
